---
title: "Curriculum Vitae"
page-layout: full
toc: true
---

# 

I am a molecular ecologist with expertise in Arctic microbial ecology, environmental DNA, molecular biology and bioinformatics. My research combines long-term ecological monitoring, high-throughput sequencing and computational analyses to investigate microbial communities across Arctic and terrestrial ecosystems.

---

# Current Position

### Postdoctoral Researcher
**The University of Mississippi, USA**

**July 2026 – Present**

Current research focuses on microbe-mineral interactions, biofertilisers and sustainable agriculture, investigating how beneficial microbial communities influence crop productivity and resilience through field and greenhouse experiments.

---

# Education

### PhD in Arctic Biology

**University Centre in Svalbard (UNIS), Norway**

Joint degree with the **University of Agder (UiA), Norway**

2019–2025

---

### Erasmus Mundus MSc — Marine Environment and Resources (MER)

- University of Bordeaux, France
- University of the Basque Country, Spain
- University of Liège, Belgium
- University of Southampton, United Kingdom
- University of Tromsø, Norway

2016–2018

---

### BSc — Zoology, Microbiology and Chemistry

St. Joseph's College (Autonomous), Bangalore, India

2013–2016

---

# Professional Experience

| Year | Position |
|------|----------|
| **2026–Present** | **Postdoctoral Researcher**, The University of Mississippi, USA |
| Mar-May2026 | Teaching assistant, Arctic Microboiology (AB327/827), UNIS |
| Mar-May2026 | Researcher, XTREAM (EU Horizon Project) |
| Oct-Nov 2025 | Teaching Assistant – Arctic Marine Molecular Ecology (AB-332/832), UNIS |
| Jun-Aug 2025 | Science Communicator, Polar Bears International |
| 2019–2025 | Doctoral Researcher, UNIS |
| 2018 | Research Assistant, CAGE, UiT – The Arctic University of Norway |

---

# Teaching & Student Mentorship

During my PhD and beyond, I contributed to undergraduate, Master's and PhD teaching through lectures, laboratory practicals, field excursions and one-to-one student mentoring.

I have mentored:

- **2 Master's students**
- **1 Bachelor's research intern**

---

# Leadership & Service

### Head of the Temporary Academic Staff

**University Centre in Svalbard (2022–2024)**

Responsibilities included:

- Representing temporary academic staff
- Organising workshops
- Participating in meetings on teaching and course organisation
- Supporting new staff integration


---

# Scholarships

- Erasmus Mundus MER Scholarship
- Aquimob Mobility Scholarship
- UPV/EHU Mobility Grant

---

# Research Outputs

## Peer-reviewed Journal Articles

1. **Chitkara, C.**, Juul-Pedersen, T., Krawczyk, D., Søreide, J. E., Vader, A., Gradinger, R., Winding, M. H. S., & Vonnahme, T. R. (2024).  
   *Seasonality in phytoplankton communities and production in three Arctic fjords across a climate gradient.*  
   **Progress in Oceanography**, 227, 103317.

2. **Vonnahme, T. R., Chitkara, C.**, Krawczyk, D., Meire, L., Skogseth, R., Vader, A., & Juul-Pedersen, T. (2025).  
   *Abrupt decline of microplankton species richness linked to coastal inflow in an Arctic fjord.*  
   **Limnology and Oceanography**, 70, 2688–2702.

3. **Sen, A.**, Himmler, T., Hong, W. L., *et al.* (2019).  
   *Atypical biological features of a new cold seep site on the Lofoten–Vesterålen continental margin (northern Norway).*  
   **Scientific Reports**, 9, 1762.

4. **Sen, A., Chitkara, C.**, Hong, W., Lepland, A., Cochrane, S., di Primio, R., & Brunstad, H. (2019).  
   *Image-based quantitative comparisons indicate heightened megabenthos diversity and abundance at a site of weak hydrocarbon seepage in the southwestern Barents Sea.*  
   **PeerJ**, 7:e7398.

---

## Cruise Reports

1. **Gerland, S., *et al.*** (2022).  
   *Seasonal Cruise Q1 2021: Cruise Report.*  
   **The Nansen Legacy Report Series**, 29/2022.

2. **CAGE-18-3 Cruise to the Barents Sea, Storfjorden Trough, East Greenland Ridge (Legs 1–2), Arctic Ocean Vestnesa Ridge and PKF (Leg 3).** (2022).  
   **CAGE – Centre for Arctic Gas Hydrate, Environment and Climate Report Series**, 6.

---

## Published Research Datasets

1. **Vader, A., Chitkara, C., Ericson, Y., Marquardt, M., Gabrielsen, T. M., Thompson, S., Chierici, M., & Søreide, J. E.** (2024).  
   *Nutrients from the marine ISA time series station in Svalbard.*

2. **Chitkara, C., Gabrielsen, T. M., Marquardt, M., & Vader, A.** (2024).  
   *Raw DNA sequences from a high-Arctic time series station in Isfjorden.*

3. **Vader, A., Chitkara, C., Marquardt, M., Søreide, J., & Gabrielsen, T. M.** (2021).  
   *ISA_Svalbard_Chlorophyll_A_2011–2019.*

---

## Manuscripts in press

- **Seasonality of planktonic microbial eukaryotic communities in Arctic versus Atlantic Spitsbergen fjords.**

---



# Research Cruises

- R/V Kronprins Haakon - The Nansen legacy seasonal cruise (Spring 2021)
- R/V Polarsyssel - Monthly fieldwork (2019-2024)
- R/V Helmer Hanssen - Student cruises (2019-2024)
- UNIS Polaris - Monthly fieldwork (2019-2024)
- Hurtigruten Expedition Cruises (Summer 2022)

---

# Outreach & Science Communication

Highlights include:

- Polar Bears International
- Pint of Science
- The Nansen Legacy
- UNIS Open Day
- Researcher's Night
- Science communication for schools and the public

More details are available on the **Outreach** (Research) and **Featured** pages.


---

# Professional Profiles

<div style="display:flex; gap:20px; flex-wrap:wrap; margin-top:20px;">

<a href="https://orcid.org/0009-0004-8429-2326" target="_blank" class="hero-button">
🟢 ORCID
</a>

<a href="https://scholar.google.fr/citations?user=OoRG4bsAAAAJ&hl=en" target="_blank" class="hero-button">
🎓 Google Scholar
</a>

</div>
---
